channel.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories
channel.description: Top stories in the U.S. and world news, politics, health, science, business, music, arts and culture. Nonprofit journalism with a mission. This is NPR.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

image.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories

item.title: Muslims begin the annual Hajj against a backdrop of war concerns
item.description: More than 1.5 million pilgrims have arrived in Saudi Arabia for the annual Hajj pilgrimage, one of the Five Pillars of Islam, against the backdrop of a tenuous ceasefire in the Iran war and related regional tensions.
item.content:encoded:
More than 1.5 million pilgrims have arrived in Saudi Arabia for the annual Hajj pilgrimage, one of the Five Pillars of Islam, against the backdrop of a tenuous ceasefire in the Iran war and related regional tensions.

(Image credit: Altaf Qadri)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: China launches Shenzhou 23 spacecraft with 1 of 3 astronauts set for yearlong stay
item.description: China launched the Shenzhou 23 spacecraft with 3 astronauts heading to its space station. One astronaut is set to stay in space for a year to explore human adaptability in long-duration spaceflights.
item.content:encoded:
China launched the Shenzhou 23 spacecraft with 3 astronauts heading to its space station. One astronaut is set to stay in space for a year to explore human adaptability in long-duration spaceflights.

(Image credit: Ng Han Guan)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: California chemical tank has cracked, causing state of emergency, thousands to evacuate
item.description: One California town is in a state of emergency and 50,000 people are under an evacuation order as a malfunctioning chemical tank at an aerospace plant is overheating and could leak or explode.
item.content:encoded:
One California town is in a state of emergency and 50,000 people are under an evacuation order as a malfunctioning chemical tank at an aerospace plant is overheating and could leak or explode.

(Image credit: Apu Gomes)
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel

item.title: DR Congo Ebola cases rise amid distrust, armed conflict zone
item.description: Africa races to contain a fast-spreading Ebola outbreak threatening 10 countries as infections spill from eastern Congo into Uganda.
item.content:encoded:
Africa races to contain a fast-spreading Ebola outbreak threatening 10 countries as infections spill from eastern Congo into Uganda.

(Image credit: Moses Sawasawa)
item.dc:creator: Emmet Livingstone

item.title: More than 500 children have died in an outbreak that the world is virtually ignoring
item.description: The number of cases — and deaths — in Bangladesh is staggering. As of Sunday, 528 have died, mostly children. How did this measles outbreak begin? And how is the country responding?
item.content:encoded:
The number of cases — and deaths — in Bangladesh is staggering. As of Sunday, 528 have died, mostly children. How did this measles outbreak begin? And how is the country responding?

(Image credit: Anik Rahman for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Ali Asif Shawon

item.title: Mind-bending photos by anonymous cousins show the pain and dreams of Afghan women
item.description: The young women make photos that look at life — how it is, how they wish it could be — under Taliban rule. The images are on display at the Photoville Festival in Brooklyn, New York.
item.content:encoded:
The young women make photos that look at life — how it is, how they wish it could be — under Taliban rule. The images are on display at the Photoville Festival in Brooklyn, New York.

(Image credit: Mahnaz Ebrahimi|January 2026)
item.dc:creator: Diane Cole

item.title: Sunday Puzzle: Two words, one anagram
item.description: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe plays the puzzle with W-U-N-C listener Thomas Hirschman of Durham, North Carolina. and Puzzlemaster Will Shortz.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe plays the puzzle with W-U-N-C listener Thomas Hirschman of Durham, North Carolina. and Puzzlemaster Will Shortz.
item.dc:creator: Will Shortz

item.title: Religious leaders, lawmakers push for $1 billion to secure houses of worship
item.description: There's an effort on Capitol Hill to increase funding for the Nonprofit Security Grant Program, which awards funding to houses of worship to harden their defenses. In 2024, roughly a third of those who applied actually received funding.
item.content:encoded:
There's an effort on Capitol Hill to increase funding for the Nonprofit Security Grant Program, which awards funding to houses of worship to harden their defenses. In 2024, roughly a third of those who applied actually received funding.

(Image credit: Photo courtesy of the Jewish Federations of North America)
item.dc:creator: Barbara Sprunt

item.title: 15 movies we can't wait to see this summer, from Spielberg to 'Spider-Man'
item.description: The Toy Story squad takes on a tablet, the Minions take on Hollywood and Christopher Nolan takes on a Greek epic.
item.content:encoded: The Toy Story squad takes on a tablet, the Minions take on Hollywood and Christopher Nolan takes on a Greek epic.
item.dc:creator: Bob Mondello

item.title: Nurse convicted in patient's death is now a national speaker on hospital safety
item.description: RaDonda Vaught was convicted of negligent homicide after dispensing the wrong drug to a patient. She now gives speeches about hospital safety in an era of automation and artificial intelligence.
item.content:encoded:
RaDonda Vaught was convicted of negligent homicide after dispensing the wrong drug to a patient. She now gives speeches about hospital safety in an era of automation and artificial intelligence.

(Image credit: Mark Humphrey)
item.dc:creator: Blake Farmer
